trace.text:
Squad selection for Friendlies Jun 09 (4-3-3, 105/120 budget, 9 nations represented, max 3 per country, bench total kept to 18):

GK: David Raya (Spain). Clean-sheet anchor — Spain host Peru as ~82% favourites (odds 1.17-1.22), with bookmakers backing a 3-0/handicap -1.5 scoreline and BTTS-No. Even with Yamal, Pedri and Nico Williams all out injured, Spain's back five (Porro, Pubill, Laporte, Grimaldo) plus Raya should comfortably contain a Peru side ranked 53rd in the world.

DEF: Pedro Porro (Spain), Nicolás Otamendi (Argentina), Igor Diveev (Russia), Mamadou Sarr (Senegal). All four are confirmed/predicted starters in matches their nations are expected to win comfortably (Spain vs Peru, Argentina vs Iceland, Russia vs Trinidad & Tobago, Senegal vs Saudi Arabia), giving a strong combined floor of clean-sheet and appearance points. Otamendi at just 5 is the standout value pick of the round — a nailed starter in a near-certain Argentina win for a fraction of the cost of comparable defenders.

MID: Alexis Mac Allister (Argentina), Dominik Szoboszlai (Hungary), Iliman Ndiaye (Senegal). Mac Allister starts centrally for an Argentina side that has scored 17 goals in five matches and kept 5 clean sheets in 6. Szoboszlai is Hungary's captain and their most dangerous creative force — lined up against a weaker Kazakhstan side, he is Hungary's primary set-piece and penalty taker, giving significantly higher expected value than a straight forward-line pick from a comparably dominant nation. Ndiaye becomes Senegal's primary attacking outlet with Sadio Mané expected to be rested ahead of their France rematch.

FWD: Ferran Torres (Spain, vice-captain), Lionel Messi (Argentina, captain), Nicolas Jackson (Senegal). Torres is the focal point of Spain's weakened-but-still-dominant front line and the most likely Spaniard to convert their territorial dominance into goals. Jackson leads the line for Senegal against a Saudi side that has lost 3 of its last 4. Messi — confirmed starting "from the off" against Iceland — gets the armband: at a squad value of just 7 he's the cheapest genuine difference-maker in the player pool, with Argentina's front line in red-hot form (17 goals in 5) and Iceland having failed to even qualify for the World Cup.

Bench: kept deliberately cheap (combined value 18, no premium player parked unused) — Lyes Hoel (San Marino GK, 1, pure filler), Kenji Cabrera (Peru MID, 5, confirmed starter who'll get 90 minutes even in a Spain landslide), Edo Kayembe (DR Congo MID, 6, confirmed starter), and Firas Al-Buraikan (Saudi Arabia FWD, 6, their first-choice striker). All four are real starters for their sides rather than expensive players sitting idle.

Captain: Messi. Vice: Ferran Torres — both nailed starters in matches their sides are strongly expected to win, maximising the 2x multiplier ceiling.

Country balance: Spain (3), Argentina (3), Senegal (3), Hungary (1), Russia (1), Peru (1), DR Congo (1), Saudi Arabia (1), San Marino (1) — concentrates picks in the round's strongest sides for the XI while spreading the bench across confirmed starters from weaker nations, respecting the 3-per-country cap throughout.

Match predictions: built around the heavy favourites covering comfortably (Spain 3-0 Peru, Argentina 3-0 Iceland, Russia 3-0 Trinidad & Tobago, Senegal 2-0 Saudi Arabia, San Marino 0-3 Azerbaijan), tighter calls for evenly matched continental derbies (Kuwait 1-1 Oman, Equatorial Guinea 1-1 Comoros), and modest away wins for the technically superior side in the remaining Asian/European/African friendlies (Hong Kong, Syria, Burkina Faso, Hungary, Armenia all backed to edge it on the road or at home based on current form and ranking gaps).
